signals.priority_reason: Strong G10 bilateral theme (spread > 1.20%) fused with Tier-1 RBNZ hike catalyst.
signals.title: NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme
signals.text: NZD outperformance accelerates following the RBNZ's first rate hike in three years, while CHF remains the G10 laggard with a -0.710% weekly average. The 250bp carry advantage and the shift to a 2.5% policy rate drive capital toward NZD/CHF, which closed Friday at 0.4643. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 46% hike priced) on 2 September is the catalyst for confirming a sustained tightening cycle.

signals.priority_reason: Significant COT positioning extreme in JPY (-104k) creating squeeze risk ahead of Tier-1 US CPI.
signals.title: USD/JPY — COT Short Squeeze Risk + US CPI Catalyst
signals.text: JPY COT net short -104,231 (29.1% long) creates massive squeeze risk for USD/JPY if upcoming US CPI data underwhelms. While the +255bp OIS carry advantage favors USD, the pair's Friday close at 161.67 remains vulnerable to a positioning unwind if yields soften. The next Fed meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 29 July determines if the carry trade remains viable against this crowded short.

signals.priority_reason: Large CAD COT short position (-87k) and strong retail short bias (80%) ahead of BoC meeting.
signals.title: USD/CAD — COT Crowding + BoC Policy Decision
signals.text: CAD COT net short -87,846 (22.6% long) and an 80% retail short bias on USD/CAD signal a heavily one-sided market ahead of Wednesday's rate decision. Despite the +124bp OIS carry favoring USD, the -2.06σ correlation break with WTI oil suggests the pair is decoupling from energy fundamentals. The next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 15 July is the catalyst for either a short-covering rally or a breakdown toward 1.40.

signals.priority_reason: EUR COT short position and negative CESI data trend.
signals.title: EUR/USD — COT Short Bias + CESI Data Underperformance
signals.text: EUR COT net short -66,057 (34.7% long) aligns with a negative CESI score of -9, reflecting persistent Eurozone data misses. The +135bp OIS carry advantage for USD and a bear steepener in the US yield curve maintain downside pressure on the 1.1419 Friday close. The next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 23 July is the catalyst for testing whether the current hold bias tilts toward a cut if the negative CESI trend and short positioning persist.

signals.priority_reason: GBP COT long position and positive 1W momentum.
signals.title: GBP/USD — COT Long Positioning + Yield Support
signals.text: GBP COT net long +17,228 (59.0% long) supports the pair's 1.3398 Friday close as it outperforms most G10 peers. A restrictive real rate of +0.78% and a narrow OIS carry spread (+20bp, favors GBP) against the USD provide a stable floor for Sterling. The next BoE meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 30 July is the catalyst for determining if the hold bias can withstand cooling inflation expectations.
